steps.title: Combine the constants
steps.content: The expression $8+d^2+3$ has two numerical constants and one $d^2$ term. Add the constants: $$8+3=11.$$ The $d^2$ term has no like term to combine with, so it stays as is.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $d^2+24$ — would need $8+3=24$, but $8+3=11$, not 24. Eliminated.
  • B) $d^2+11$ — matches $8+3=11$ exactly.
  • C) $d^2+5$ — would require subtracting, but the expression adds the constants. Eliminated.
  • D) $d^2-11$ — the sign is wrong; $8+3$ is positive 11. Eliminated.
That matches Choice B — ${d}^{2}+11$.
